PRs missing backports.title: Fix typos and grammar errors across documentation
PRs missing backports.body:
Fix 77 typos and grammar errors in 39 documentation files.

**Categories of fixes:**

- **Spelling errors**: "seperately" → "separately", "preveiously" → "previously"
- **Subject-verb agreement**: "functions uses" → "functions use", "protocol require" → "protocol requires", "names takes" → "names take"
- **Missing words**: "if there no" → "if there is no", "must one of" → "must be one of", "are longer called" → "are no longer called"
- **Extra words**: "effects with involving" → "effects involving", "whose the result" → "whose result", "the its behavior" → "its behavior"
- **Wrong prepositions**: "obey to the following" → "obey the following", "equals to" → "equals"
- **Wrong word forms**: "fallback on" → "fall back on", "In the later case" → "In the latter case", "most noticeable" → "most notably"
- **Grammatical fixes**: "allows to run" → "allows you to run", "equivalent to call" → "equivalent to calling"
- **Duplicated text**: "a lock a lock" → "a lock", "next the timer next fires" → "the timer next fires"
- **Miscellaneous**: double space, missing space before parenthesis, American English spelling ("behaviour" → "behavior")

**Files changed by section:**

| Section | Files | Fixes |
|---------|-------|-------|
| `Doc/tutorial/` | 5 | 7 |
| `Doc/c-api/` | 7 | 13 |
| `Doc/library/` | 22 | 52 |
| Misc (`glossary`, `bugs`, `copyright`, `license`, `deprecations`) | 5 | 5 |
| **Total** | **39** | **77** |

<!-- readthedocs-preview cpython-previews start -->
----
📚 Documentation preview 📚: https://cpython-previews--144709.org.readthedocs.build/

<!-- readthedocs-preview cpython-previews end -->

PRs missing backports.title: gh-123471: Make concurrent iteration over itertools.permutations and itertools.combinations_with_replacement thread-safe
PRs missing backports.body:
We use a critical section similar to the approach in https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/132814.

PRs missing backports.title: bpo-40243: Use numeric_changed for UCD.numeric
PRs missing backports.body:
This was causing `ucd_3_2_0.numeric()` to pick up only decimal
changes between Unicode 3.2.0 and the current version.

PRs missing backports.title: gh-144601: Avoid sharing exception objects raised in a `PyInit` function across multiple interpreters
gh-130595: Keep traceback alive for WITH_EXCEPT_START
PRs missing backports.body:
The WITH_EXCEPT_START opcode is getting the traceback from the exception object and then passing it down via a vectorcall, but only after decref'ing the traceback. 

But the exception object can have the only reference to the traceback. If the receiver of the traceback does something to cause the exception's trace back to be replaced, then uses the traceback, they access dangling pointer. The traceback shouldn't solely be kept alive by the exception object which can be mutated.

<!-- gh-issue-number: gh-130595 -->
* Issue: gh-130595
<!-- /gh-issue-number -->

PRs missing backports.title: gh-130094: Fix race conditions in `importlib`
PRs missing backports.body:
Entries may be added or removed from `sys.meta_path` concurrently. For example, setuptools temporarily adds and removes the `distutils` finder from the beginning of the list. The local copy ensures that we don't skip over any entries.

Some packages modify `sys.modules` during import. For example, `collections` inserts the entry for `collections.abc`  into `sys.modules` during import. We need to ensure that we re-check `sys.modules` *after* the parent module is fully initialized.


<!-- gh-issue-number: gh-130094 -->
* Issue: gh-130094
<!-- /gh-issue-number -->

PRs missing backports.title: gh-131261: expat/refresh.sh: Expand list of manual steps
PRs missing backports.body:
There's a few manual steps for updating expat; here's a list.
(This is a separate PR as it doesn't need backports.)

We could run `make regen-sbom` automatically, but it's probably better to do it after checking `git diff`.
